scenes.narration: Welcome to the Cloud Native workshop labs on GitHub Codespaces. Your whole session runs in one codespace: a real VS Code editor, Docker, and a two node Kubernetes cluster you create with kind. You install nothing on your computer. In this first lab we build a microservice, containerize it, and ship it to a registry.

Lab zero: create the hello c n c f repo, open a codespace on main, verify Docker, install kubectl and kind, then create a two node kind cluster. Untaint the control plane so pods can schedule on both nodes — that makes load balancing visible in Lab 2.

Create hello c n c f: a tiny Flask service designed so the platform becomes visible. It returns served by, the hostname answering each request, a version field, and a health endpoint Kubernetes will probe.

Build the image, run it, prove isolation with a second container on port eighty eighty one, then log in to Docker Hub, tag with your username, and push. Commit and push your files — Codespaces authenticates git automatically.
Recap: in one codespace you built a purpose designed microservice, containerized it, proved isolation, and shipped a versioned image. Stay here — Lab 2 begins in this exact terminal.
